text: If we had implemented the system, we would not be dealing with errors today.

Marcus, do you remember when we used to track everything on spreadsheets?
Of course. Every Monday, I would spend two hours compiling the data by hand. It was painfully slow.
The transition to the automated dashboard was hard at first. I really struggled to get used to the new interface.
Same here. But once I got used to the new tools, I became a big advocate. Now I cannot imagine going back to the legacy system.
The biggest change was the mindset. We used to wait for problems. Now we anticipate them. That overhaul changed everything.
Absolutely. We streamlined and optimized so much. The supply chain has really evolved. Are you fully used to all the new processes now?
We used to rely on manual spreadsheets for tracking.
Every Monday, someone would compile the data by hand.
It took time to get used to the new automated system.
Now I am used to working with the real-time dashboard.
Our supply chain has evolved from reactive to proactive.

Fabiana, I have been thinking about how much our supply chain has evolved over the past three years. We used to rely entirely on manual tracking spreadsheets. Every Monday, someone would compile the data by hand and email it to the team. It was painfully slow. Now we have an automated dashboard that updates in real time. At first, I struggled to get used to the new interface, but now I cannot imagine going back. We also used to work with just two regional vendors. We would place the same orders quarter after quarter without questioning the terms. That has completely changed. We have streamlined our vendor selection process and now we evaluate performance quarterly. The shift from reactive to proactive management has been the biggest transformation. We used to wait for problems to happen. Now we anticipate them. It took time to get used to this mindset, but the results speak for themselves.

Hi team, I wanted to share some thoughts on how our processes have evolved. When I first joined Bloomberg, we used to handle customs documentation manually. It would take three to four days to process each shipment. Now, with the digital platform, we have cut that down to twenty-four hours. I remember struggling to get used to the automated system at first. The transition was not easy, but it was worth it. Another major change is vendor communication. We used to rely on weekly phone calls with our suppliers. Now we have a real-time collaboration platform. Marcus would always say that the old way worked fine, but once he got used to the new tools, he became the biggest advocate. The key lesson is that adaptation is not optional in global supply chains. We used to think of change as disruptive. Now we see it as a competitive advantage. If you are still getting used to any of the new processes, reach out. We are all in this together.

Bloomberg used to rely on manual tracking spreadsheets. The team would compile data by hand every Monday. They transitioned to an automated real-time dashboard. At first, people struggled to get used to the new system. Now the shift from reactive to proactive management is complete.
